title: CLT Net Salary Calculator
meta_title: CLT Net Salary Calculator 2026 | INSS & IRRF - CalcForgeTools
meta_description: Calculate your Brazilian CLT net salary instantly. See exact INSS and IRRF deductions using the official 2026 tax tables and find out your take-home pay.

hero.title: CLT Net Salary Calculator
hero.subtitle: Find out exactly how much lands in your bank account. Enter your gross salary and see INSS, IRRF deductions, and FGTS deposits calculated automatically with the 2026 tables.

formula: Net = Gross - INSS (progressive) - IRRF (progressive) - Other Deductions
formula_explanation: The calculation uses the official 2026 progressive INSS and IRRF tax tables. INSS is applied in bands on the gross salary. IRRF is calculated on the base (gross - INSS - R$189.59 per dependent deduction).
example: With a gross salary of R$ 4,000.00 and no dependents, the INSS deduction is approximately R$ 373.09 and the IRRF is approximately R$ 73.83, resulting in a net salary of around R$ 3,553.08.

faq.question: What is the difference between gross and net salary?
faq.answer: Gross salary is the total amount defined in your employment contract. Net salary is the amount that actually hits your bank account after mandatory INSS and IRRF deductions.

faq.question: What is FGTS and is it deducted from salary?
faq.answer: FGTS (Severance Indemnity Fund) is an 8% deposit made by the employer into a linked worker account. It is NOT deducted from your salary — it is an additional employer obligation.

faq.question: How much does each dependent reduce in taxes?
faq.answer: Each dependent reduces R$ 189.59 from the IRRF calculation base. This means having dependents lowers the amount on which income tax is calculated, reducing the final deduction.
